meta.title: India Job Market 2023-24
meta.primarySource: PLFS Annual Report 2023-24 (July 2023–June 2024), Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation (MoSPI), Government of India, Published September 2024
meta.notes: PLFS uses NIC-2008 industrial classification. 'Other Services' (11.5%) is disaggregated into IT, Education, Healthcare, Government, Creative & Media, and Personal Services using sector-specific official sources. Worker counts are estimates based on WPR × population data.

sectors.name: Agriculture & Allied
sources: PLFS Annual Report 2023-24, Table A-1, MoSPI
Economic Survey 2023-24, Chapter 8, Ministry of Finance

occupations.name: Crop & Field Farming
occupations.description: Cultivators and farmers growing food, cash and commercial crops. Bulk of rural self-employed workforce.
sources: PLFS 2023-24, Table 23 — usual-status workers in crop production
ICAR Annual Report 2023-24, Indian Council of Agricultural Research
Agricultural Census 2021-22, Department of Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare (DAFQ)

occupations.name: Animal Husbandry & Dairy
occupations.description: Livestock rearing, poultry, dairy farming. India is world's largest milk producer (239MT, 2023-24).
sources: DAHD Annual Report 2023-24, Dept. of Animal Husbandry & Dairying, MoFAHD
NDDB Annual Report 2023-24, National Dairy Development Board

occupations.name: Fisheries & Aquaculture
occupations.description: Marine and inland fisheries, aquaculture. India is 3rd largest fish producer globally.
sources: Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana Progress Report 2024, DoF

occupations.name: Agricultural Wage Labour
occupations.description: Landless casual agricultural labourers. Declining as mechanisation increases.
sources: PLFS 2023-24, Table A-12 — casual labour in agriculture

occupations.name: Horticulture & Plantation
occupations.description: Fruits, vegetables, floriculture, spices, tea, coffee plantations. High-growth export segment.
sources: NHB Annual Report 2023-24, National Horticulture Board
Second Advance Estimates of Horticulture 2023-24, NHB

sectors.name: Construction
sources: NSO GDP Estimates 2023-24, MoSPI (Construction GDP growth 9.9% in FY24)

occupations.name: General Construction Workers
occupations.description: Masons, carpenters, painters, site labourers. Demand driven by PM Awas Yojana, infra push.
sources: Building & Other Construction Workers Act data, Ministry of Labour 2024

occupations.name: Electricians & Plumbers
occupations.description: Skilled trades in growing demand from residential, industrial and solar installations.
sources: NSDC (National Skill Development Corporation) Skill Gap Study 2024
ITI (Industrial Training Institute) Enrolment Data, DGT 2023-24

occupations.name: Construction Supervisors & Foremen
occupations.description: Site supervisors, project coordinators overseeing residential and commercial builds.
sources: CREDAI (Confederation of Real Estate Developers) India Report 2024

occupations.name: Civil & Structural Engineers
occupations.description: Degree-level engineers in construction, infrastructure (roads, bridges, metros, airports).
sources: AICTE All India Survey on Higher Education 2023-24
MoRTH Annual Report 2023-24 (17,840 km of highways built in FY24)

occupations.name: Real Estate Agents & Brokers
occupations.description: Property sales agents registered under RERA. Residential market at record ₹3.47L cr in FY24.
sources: RERA (Real Estate Regulatory Authority) Registration Data 2024
Knight Frank India Real Estate Market Review H2 2024

occupations.name: Heavy Equipment Operators
occupations.description: Crane, excavator, JCB and bulldozer operators. Demand from NIP (National Infrastructure Pipeline ₹111L cr).
sources: PLFS 2023-24, plant and machine operators in construction
NIP Progress Report FY24, DEA, Ministry of Finance

sectors.name: Trade, Hotels & Restaurants
sources: NITI Aayog India's Booming Gig & Platform Economy 2022

occupations.name: Retail Shop Workers
occupations.description: Owners and employees of kiranas, retail outlets, showrooms. ~14M kirana stores in India.
sources: Retailers Association of India (RAI) Annual Report 2024

occupations.name: Restaurant & Hotel Staff
occupations.description: Chefs, waitstaff, housekeeping. Hospitality recovering strongly post-COVID; revenue ₹3.4L cr FY24.
sources: FHRAI (Federation of Hotels & Restaurants Assoc. India) Annual Survey 2024

occupations.name: Wholesale & Distribution
occupations.description: Wholesale merchants, distributors, FMCG channel partners.

occupations.name: E-commerce & Online Retail
occupations.description: Warehouse pickers, online seller support, marketplace operations. India e-commerce $70Bn FY24.

occupations.name: Food & Package Delivery (Gig)
occupations.description: Swiggy, Zomato, Zepto, Blinkit, Dunzo delivery partners. Fastest-growing informal employment.
sources: NITI Aayog: India's Booming Gig & Platform Economy, 2022 (est. 7.7M gig workers by 2024-25)
Swiggy, Zomato partner count disclosures in DRHP/annual reports 2024

occupations.name: Tourism & Travel Agents
occupations.description: Travel agents, tour operators, guides. Domestic tourism crossed 2.5Bn trips FY24.

occupations.name: Street Vendors & Hawkers
occupations.description: Street food vendors, mobile hawkers. PM SVANidhi scheme has registered 6.8M vendors.
sources: PM SVANidhi Dashboard, Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs, April 2024
NASVI (National Assoc. of Street Vendors of India) census data 2023

occupations.name: Garment & Textile Workers
occupations.description: Apparel stitching, spinning, weaving. India's textile exports $35Bn FY24; 2nd largest employer in manufacturing.
sources: Confederation of Indian Textile Industry (CITI) Annual Survey 2024

occupations.name: Food Processing Workers
occupations.description: Packaged food, beverages, dairy processing, milling. Sector grew 7.4% in FY24.
sources: Ministry of Food Processing Industries Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Automobile & Auto Components
occupations.description: Vehicle assembly, auto parts, EV components. India became 3rd largest auto market in 2023.
sources: SIAM (Society of Indian Automobile Manufacturers) Annual Report 2023-24
ACMA (Auto Component Manufacturers Assoc.) Industry Survey 2024

occupations.name: Electronics Assembly
occupations.description: Mobile phones, consumer electronics assembly. PLI scheme boosted production; Apple, Samsung scale up.
sources: ICEA (India Cellular & Electronics Association) Annual Report 2024
MeitY PLI for Large Scale Electronics progress report 2024

occupations.name: Chemical & Pharmaceutical Workers
occupations.description: API manufacturing, formulations, specialty chemicals. India = 'Pharmacy of the World', $28Bn exports FY24.
sources: Pharmexcil Annual Report 2023-24, Pharmaceuticals Export Promotion Council

occupations.name: MSME Workers (General)
occupations.description: Workers across 63M registered MSMEs covering diverse light manufacturing sub-sectors.
sources: MSME Annual Report 2023-24, Ministry of MSME
Udyam Registration Portal data, April 2024 (6.3Cr MSMEs registered)

occupations.name: Skilled Craft & Artisans
occupations.description: Handicrafts, handlooms, pottery, jewellery making. Heritage crafts supported by GI tagging.
sources: Development Commissioner for Handicrafts Annual Report 2023-24, Ministry of Textiles
Office of DC Handlooms, Ministry of Textiles, 2023-24

sectors.name: IT & Technology
sectors.plfsCode: NIC J (subset of Other Services, PLFS 2023-24)
sources: NASSCOM Strategic Review 2025 (5.8M direct IT-BPM employees)

occupations.name: Software Developers & Engineers
occupations.description: India's 5.8M IT workforce exports $194Bn (FY24). Software engineers are the core — Java, Python, cloud-native stacks.
sources: NASSCOM Strategic Review 2025 — IT-BPM direct employment 5.8M
QS/Times Higher Education India STEM Graduate Output 2024

occupations.name: IT Support & BPO / ITeS
occupations.description: Customer support, data entry, back-office processing. BPO industry $38Bn revenue FY24 but Gen-AI poses high disruption risk.

occupations.name: Data Scientists & Analysts
occupations.description: ML modellers, analytics leads, BI developers. India has the world's 3rd largest analytics talent pool.
sources: LinkedIn Jobs on the Rise India 2024

occupations.name: Cybersecurity Professionals
occupations.description: Severe global shortage: 4M unfilled positions. India needs 1M cybersecurity pros by 2025 (DSCI).
sources: DSCI (Data Security Council of India) India Cybersecurity Report 2024

occupations.name: Cloud & DevOps Engineers
occupations.description: AWS/Azure/GCP certified engineers managing cloud infra. India hosts largest AWS skill certification base outside US.

occupations.name: AI / ML Engineers
occupations.description: Highest-paid and fastest-growing role. LLM fine-tuning, GenAI product development, MLOps.
sources: LinkedIn Jobs on the Rise India 2024

occupations.name: Product Managers (Tech)
occupations.description: Tech PMs at India's 112 unicorns and global tech centres. Top-paying non-engineering role.
sources: NASSCOM Startup Report 2025 (112 unicorns, 3,000+ active startups)

occupations.name: UI/UX Designers
occupations.description: Product designers for apps, web, enterprise software. Growing with India's SaaS and consumer internet boom.

sectors.name: Education
sectors.plfsCode: NIC P (subset of Other Services, PLFS 2023-24)
sources: UDISE+ 2024-25, Department of School Education & Literacy (DSEL), MoE — 10.12M school teachers
AISHE 2023-24, Dept. of Higher Education, MoE — 1.6M college/university faculty

occupations.name: School Teachers (Primary & Secondary)
occupations.description: 10.12M teachers across 14.7L govt and private schools. 50% govt school teachers median ₹16K; private school median ₹28K.
sources: UDISE+ Flash Statistics 2024-25, DSEL, MoE — 1.47M schools, 10.12M teachers

occupations.name: College & University Faculty
occupations.description: Professors, associate and assistant professors in 1,168 universities and 45,473 colleges.
sources: AISHE 2023-24 Report, Dept. of Higher Education, MoE

occupations.name: EdTech Professionals
occupations.description: Content creators, instructors, LMS developers at BYJU'S, Unacademy, PhysicsWallah and 4,500+ EdTech startups.

sectors.name: Healthcare
sectors.plfsCode: NIC Q (subset of Other Services, PLFS 2023-24)
sources: Indian Nursing Council Annual Report 2023-24: 2.1M registered nurses

occupations.name: Doctors (Allopathic, MBBS+)
occupations.description: MBBS, MD, specialist doctors across public and private hospitals. 1 doctor per 834 population (NMC 2024).
sources: National Medical Commission (NMC) Annual Report 2023-24 — 1.3M registered allopathic doctors

occupations.name: Nurses & Midwives
occupations.description: India's largest regulated healthcare workforce. Global demand opens overseas migration pathways.
sources: Indian Nursing Council Annual Report 2023-24 — 2.1M registered nurses
MoHFW Human Resources for Health Report 2024

occupations.name: Pharmacists
occupations.description: Retail pharmacy, hospital dispensing, drug quality testing. India has 6.5L+ registered pharmacists.
sources: Pharmacy Council of India (PCI) Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Paramedics & Lab Technicians
occupations.description: Pathology lab techs, radiology techs, EMTs, physiotherapists. Diagnostics market growing 15% p.a.

occupations.name: ASHA & Community Health Workers
occupations.description: Front-line community health workers under NHM. 10.5L ASHA workers; India's primary rural healthcare backbone.
sources: MoHFW National Health Mission Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Hospital Administrators & Managers
occupations.description: Hospital operations, health informatics, revenue cycle management. Growing with private hospital chains.
sources: AHPI (Assoc. of Healthcare Providers India) data 2024

occupations.name: Truck & Commercial Drivers
occupations.description: Trucking moves 65% of India's freight. Chronic driver shortage (22L deficit per AIMTC) supports wages.
sources: AIMTC (All India Motor Transport Congress) Manpower Survey 2024
MoRTH Road Transport Yearbook 2022-23 (55M registered vehicles)

occupations.name: Auto-Rickshaw & App Cab Drivers
occupations.description: Ola, Uber, Rapido, Namma Yatri, BEST, city auto drivers. 5M+ on app platforms; gig classification debate ongoing.

occupations.name: Indian Railways Staff
occupations.description: India's largest employer: 1.4M employees. Loco pilots, TTEs, trackmen, station masters, workshop technicians.
sources: Indian Railways Annual Report 2023-24, Ministry of Railways (₹2.62L cr capex)

occupations.name: Logistics & Warehouse Workers
occupations.description: Fastest growing segment. PM Gati Shakti + warehousing parks push. India logistics market $250Bn by 2026.
sources: PM Gati Shakti Progress Report FY24, DPIIT

occupations.name: Aviation (Pilots, Crew & Ground)
occupations.description: India to become 3rd largest aviation market by 2025; 150M passengers FY24. Severe pilot shortage.
sources: DGCA Annual Report 2023-24 (6,285 licensed pilots; 150M pax)
MoCA (Ministry of Civil Aviation) UDAN scheme 2024

occupations.name: Postal & Courier Workers
occupations.description: India Post's 5.64L employees + private courier (Delhivery, Ecom Express, Blue Dart) 1M+ workers.
sources: India Post Annual Report 2023-24 (1.59L post offices)
DPIIT Logistics Division courier sector data 2024

occupations.name: Bank Employees
occupations.description: PSBs + private + RRBs + co-operative banks. SBI alone has 2.32L employees; HDFC Bank 1.85L.
sources: SBI, HDFC Bank, PNB annual reports FY2024

occupations.name: Insurance Agents & Professionals
occupations.description: LIC + private insurer agents. India's insurance penetration 4.2% GDP (2023); large underpenetrated market.

occupations.name: FinTech Professionals
occupations.description: India's FinTech ecosystem: $31Bn cumulative funding, 10,000+ startups. UPI processed 13Bn transactions/month.

occupations.name: Chartered Accountants & Auditors
occupations.description: 4L ICAI members + student pool. Company audit, tax, GST compliance, M&A advisory.
sources: ICAI (Institute of Chartered Accountants of India) Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Stock Brokers & Investment Advisors
occupations.description: SEBI-registered brokers, research analysts, mutual fund distributors. Demat accounts hit 15.8Cr in FY24.

occupations.name: Microfinance & Rural Banking
occupations.description: MFI loan officers, BC (Business Correspondent) agents. GLP ₹3.5L cr; 7.3Cr active borrowers.
sources: MFIN (Microfinance Institutions Network) Micrometer Report Q4 FY24
PMJDY — Jan Dhan Yojana BC network data April 2024

sectors.name: Government & Public Sector
sectors.plfsCode: NIC O (subset of Other Services, PLFS 2023-24)
sources: 7th Central Pay Commission Report (30L central govt employees)
DPE Annual Survey of Central PSEs 2023-24

occupations.name: Central & State Government Officers
occupations.description: IAS/IPS/IRS officers + Grade B/C officers in central and 28 state governments.
sources: 7th CPC Report, GoI — 32L central govt civilian employees
DoP&T Annual Report 2023-24 (Dept. of Personnel & Training)

occupations.name: Police & Paramilitary Forces
occupations.description: State police (21L) + CAPF (BSF, CRPF, CISF, SSB, ITBP, AR) 10.3L personnel.
sources: MHA Police Organisations in India 2022-23 — 21.42L sanctioned state police

occupations.name: Municipal & Urban Local Body Workers
occupations.description: Sanitation, water supply, street maintenance workers across 4,000+ ULBs (Urban Local Bodies).
sources: MoHUA (Ministry of Housing & Urban Affairs) Annual Report 2023-24
Census 2011 Urban Employment Data (latest official count)

occupations.name: PSU Employees (Central Public Sector)
occupations.description: Employees of 256 Central PSEs (ONGC, SAIL, NTPC, HAL etc.). Workforce declining as PSEs divest.
sources: DPE Annual Survey of Central Public Sector Enterprises 2023-24 — 9.36L employees

sectors.name: Personal & Domestic Services
sectors.plfsCode: NIC T–U (subset of Other Services, PLFS 2023-24)
sources: PLFS 2023-24 household activities and personal services
ILO India Domestic Workers Sector Brief 2023

occupations.name: Household Domestic Workers
occupations.description: Maids, cooks, nannies, drivers in private households. Mostly informal, women-dominated workforce.
sources: ILO India Domestic Workers Sector Brief 2023 (4–7M estimate)

occupations.name: Security Guards
occupations.description: India has the world's largest private security industry: 90L guards employed by 15,000+ agencies.
sources: ASSOCHAM Private Security Industry Report 2024 — 90L guards, ₹75K cr market
PSARA (Private Security Agencies Regulation Act) licensing data 2024

occupations.name: Beauty, Wellness & Fitness
occupations.description: Salons, spas, gyms. India beauty & wellness market ₹1.5L cr growing 12% YoY.

occupations.name: Sanitation & Waste Management
occupations.description: Municipal sweepers, garbage collectors, sewage workers. Swachh Bharat Mission expanded this workforce.
sources: Swachh Bharat Mission Urban 2.0 Progress Report FY24, MoHUA

sectors.name: Creative & Media
sectors.plfsCode: NIC R–S (subset of Other Services, PLFS 2023-24)

occupations.name: Film, TV & OTT Professionals
occupations.description: India produces 1,500+ films/year. OTT platforms (Netflix, Prime, JioHotstar) adding original content demand.
sources: FICCI-EY M&E Report 2024 — ₹2.32L cr M&E industry
CBFC (Central Board of Film Certification) Annual Report 2023-24

occupations.name: Digital Content Creators & Influencers
occupations.description: YouTube, Instagram, Moj, Josh, ShareChat content creators. India has 3rd largest creator economy globally.
sources: YouTube India 100M+ monthly active channels data 2024
Redseer Creator Economy India 2024 — 80M+ active creators

occupations.name: Journalists & Print/Digital Media
occupations.description: Print declining; digital growing. Wire services, digital news desks growing while circulation journalists decline.
sources: Press Council of India Annual Report 2023-24
FICCI-EY M&E Report 2024 — print segment contraction

occupations.name: Advertising & Marketing Professionals
occupations.description: Ad agencies, brand managers, performance marketers. India digital ad market ₹60,000 cr FY24 growing 25%.
sources: IAMAI India Digital Advertising Report FY2024
GroupM This Year Next Year India 2024 — ₹1.35L cr total ad market

occupations.name: Coal Miners
occupations.description: CIL (Coal India Limited) employs 2.87L; private coal block workers. India target 1.5BT coal by 2030.
sources: Coal India Limited Annual Report 2023-24 (2.87L employees, 773MT production)

occupations.name: Stone & Sand Quarry Workers
occupations.description: Granite, marble, sand and aggregate extraction; largely informal, hazardous work.

occupations.name: Mining Engineers & Geologists
occupations.description: Mine planners, geologists for critical minerals (lithium, cobalt, REEs) for EV and defence.
sources: Ministry of Mines Critical Minerals Strategy 2024

occupations.name: Oil & Gas Workers
occupations.description: ONGC, OIL, RIL, Cairn India upstream exploration and production workers.
sources: DGH (Directorate General of Hydrocarbons) India Petroleum Statistics 2024

occupations.name: Mineral Processing Workers
occupations.description: Iron ore beneficiation, bauxite processing, smelting. Linked to steel, aluminium sectors.

occupations.name: Power Plant & Grid Workers
occupations.description: Thermal, hydro, nuclear plant operators plus transmission & distribution workers. India's installed capacity 950GW target by 2030.
sources: CEA Annual Report 2023-24 (installed capacity 940GW FY24, target 950GW by 2030)

occupations.name: Renewable Energy Workers (Solar & Wind)
occupations.description: India's solar capacity 90GW (Mar 2024), targeting 500GW by 2030. Fastest-growing utility employment segment.
sources: MNRE Annual Report 2023-24 (90GW solar, 46GW wind installed)

occupations.name: Water Supply & Utility Workers
occupations.description: Jal Jeevan Mission targets tap water to 19Cr rural households; creating large water utility workforce.
sources: JJM (Jal Jeevan Mission) Dashboard April 2024 — 14.2Cr connections made

occupations.name: Gas Distribution & Pipeline Workers
occupations.description: City Gas Distribution (CGD) networks expanding. 630+ districts covered under CGD policy.
sources: PNGRB (Petroleum & Natural Gas Regulatory Board) Annual Report 2023-24
MoPNG (Ministry of Petroleum & Natural Gas) Annual Report 2023-24
